_note: ROLL-VERDICT OVERRIDE TEST (new hook 0x30F4A7 = 'mov r10d,eax' right after the single VM avoidance roll 0x30FA34 returns). eax IS the hit/miss verdict in REAL code (next instr: test eax,eax; je miss). This test forces eax=0 (MISS) on a normally-GUARANTEED hit to prove eax is the master verdict switch AND that it gates the entire apply/damage path. Method: RollVerdictControl ForceVerdict=0, TargetCharId=-1 (any acting unit), MaxWrites=1, so the FIRST avoidance roll of the battle is forced to miss. The pre-clamp 0x30A66F runs in OBSERVE-ONLY (LogOnly=true): on a forced miss the apply path is SKIPPED, so the pre-clamp must NOT fire for that attack (this is the NEW signal output-control could never show - output-control zeroed the debit while the apply path still RAN; here the path is skipped entirely). The result selector is observe-only. RUN: relaunch the game (asm bakes immediates at mod load), then make Agrias->Ramza (front-on, normally 100% to hit) the FIRST attack of the battle. PASS = (1) [ROLL-VERDICT event=.. native=1(hit) final=0(miss) [VERDICT FORCED]] for the acting unit, (2) Ramza takes 0 damage / HP unchanged, (3) NO pre-clamp would-write line for that attack (apply path skipped). FAIL = Ramza takes the hit (eax override did not flip the branch) OR the pre-clamp still fires (eax does not gate the apply path). Either way the ROLL-VERDICT log tells us native vs final per roll. Safe: one guarded register override on the first roll; requires a fresh launch.
